title: Drinking 5 Cups of Coffee a Day Could Reduce Heart Risk - Slashdot
excerpt: A new American Heart Association scientific statement concludes that up to about 400 milligrams a day, or roughly three to five cups of plain coffee, is safe for most adults and may be linked to lower risks of cardiovascular disease. The benefits appear to depend heavily on the source and preparatio...
content_html:
A new American Heart Association scientific statement concludes that up to about 400 milligrams a day, or roughly three to five cups of plain coffee, is safe for most adults and may be linked to lower risks of cardiovascular disease. The benefits appear to depend heavily on the source and preparation, with coffee and tea looking more favorable than energy drinks, and added sugar, cream, syrups, or sweeteners potentially canceling out the upside. ScienceAlert reports: "Caffeine consumed in coffee is a key part of daily life for millions of people," says Gregory Marcus, cardiologist at the University of California, San Francisco, and Chair of the AHA volunteer writing group behind the statement. "In our review of the most recent research, for most adults, intake of up to 400 milligrams of caffeine per day, the equivalent of up to five cups of caffeinated coffee per day without added sugars or fillers, is safe and does not increase cardiovascular risk."

The statement focused on caffeine's relationship with cardiovascular risk factors, such as blood pressure and diabetes, as well as types of cardiovascular disease, including arrhythmias, coronary artery disease, stroke, and heart failure. The picture that emerges is complicated, but generally positive. [...] All up, the new AHA statement concludes that there's a growing body of evidence that caffeine isn't harmful when taken in moderation, and that coffee specifically may be beneficial.

The statement was published in the journal Circulation.
content_text: A new American Heart Association scientific statement concludes that up to about 400 milligrams a day, or roughly three to five cups of plain coffee, is safe for most adults and may be linked to lower risks of cardiovascular disease. The benefits appear to depend heavily on the source and preparation, with coffee and tea looking more favorable than energy drinks, and added sugar, cream, syrups, or sweeteners potentially canceling out the upside. ScienceAlert reports: "Caffeine consumed in coffee is a key part of daily life for millions of people," says Gregory Marcus, cardiologist at the University of California, San Francisco, and Chair of the AHA volunteer writing group behind the statement. "In our review of the most recent research, for most adults, intake of up to 400 milligrams of caffeine per day, the equivalent of up to five cups of caffeinated coffee per day without added sugars or fillers, is safe and does not increase cardiovascular risk." The statement focused on caffeine's relationship with cardiovascular risk factors, such as blood pressure and diabetes, as well as types of cardiovascular disease, including arrhythmias, coronary artery disease, stroke, and heart failure. The picture that emerges is complicated, but generally positive. [...] All up, the new AHA statement concludes that there's a growing body of evidence that caffeine isn't harmful when taken in moderation, and that coffee specifically may be beneficial. The statement was published in the journal Circulation.

A new American Heart Association scientific statement concludes that up to about 400 milligrams a day, or roughly three to five cups of plain coffee, is safe for most adults and may be linked to lower risks of cardiovascular disease. The benefits appear to depend heavily on the source and preparation, with coffee and tea looking more favorable than energy drinks, and added sugar, cream, syrups, or sweeteners potentially canceling out the upside. ScienceAlert reports: "Caffeine consumed in coffee is a key part of daily life for millions of people," says Gregory Marcus, cardiologist at the University of California, San Francisco, and Chair of the AHA volunteer writing group behind the statement. "In our review of the most recent research, for most adults, intake of up to 400 milligrams of caffeine per day, the equivalent of up to five cups of caffeinated coffee per day without added sugars or fillers, is safe and does not increase cardiovascular risk."

The statement focused on caffeine's relationship with cardiovascular risk factors, such as blood pressure and diabetes, as well as types of cardiovascular disease, including arrhythmias, coronary artery disease, stroke, and heart failure. The picture that emerges is complicated, but generally positive. [...] All up, the new AHA statement concludes that there's a growing body of evidence that caffeine isn't harmful when taken in moderation, and that coffee specifically may be beneficial.

sentences.en: A new American Heart Association scientific statement concludes that up to about 400 milligrams a day, or roughly three to five cups of plain coffee, is safe for most adults and may be linked to lower risks of cardiovascular disease.
sentences.zh: 美国心脏协会的一项新科学声明得出结论，每天最多约 400 毫克，或大约三到五杯纯咖啡，对大多数成年人来说是安全的，并且可能与降低心血管疾病的风险有关。

sentences.en: The benefits appear to depend heavily on the source and preparation, with coffee and tea looking more favorable than energy drinks, and added sugar, cream, syrups, or sweeteners potentially canceling out the upside.
sentences.zh: 这些好处似乎在很大程度上取决于来源和制备方法，咖啡和茶看起来比能量饮料更受欢迎，而添加的糖、奶油、糖浆或甜味剂可能会抵消其优势。

sentences.en: ScienceAlert reports: "Caffeine consumed in coffee is a key part of daily life for millions of people," says Gregory Marcus, cardiologist at the University of California, San Francisco, and Chair of the AHA volunteer writing group behind the statement.
sentences.zh: ScienceAlert 报道称：“咖啡中的咖啡因是数百万人日常生活的重要组成部分，”该声明背后的美国心脏协会志愿者写作小组主席、加州大学旧金山分校心脏病专家格雷戈里·马库斯 (Gregory Marcus) 说道。

sentences.en: "In our review of the most recent research, for most adults, intake of up to 400 milligrams of caffeine per day, the equivalent of up to five cups of caffeinated coffee per day without added sugars or fillers, is safe and does not increase cardiovascular risk."
sentences.zh: “在我们对最新研究的回顾中，对于大多数成年人来说，每天摄入最多 400 毫克咖啡因，相当于每天最多五杯不添加糖或填充物的含咖啡因咖啡，是安全的，不会增加心血管风险。”

sentences.en: The statement focused on caffeine's relationship with cardiovascular risk factors, such as blood pressure and diabetes, as well as types of cardiovascular disease, including arrhythmias, coronary artery disease, stroke, and heart failure.
sentences.zh: 该声明重点关注咖啡因与心血管危险因素（如血压和糖尿病）以及心血管疾病类型（包括心律失常、冠状动脉疾病、中风和心力衰竭）的关系。

sentences.en: The picture that emerges is complicated, but generally positive. [...]
sentences.zh: 出现的情况很复杂，但总体上是积极的。 [...]

sentences.en: All up, the new AHA statement concludes that there's a growing body of evidence that caffeine isn't harmful when taken in moderation, and that coffee specifically may be beneficial.
sentences.zh: 总而言之，美国心脏协会的新声明得出的结论是，越来越多的证据表明，适量摄入咖啡因并无害处，而且咖啡可能特别有益。

sentences.en: The statement was published in the journal Circulation.
sentences.zh: 该声明发表在《循环》杂志上。

blocks.html_en: The statement focused on caffeine's relationship with cardiovascular risk factors, such as blood pressure and diabetes, as well as types of cardiovascular disease, including arrhythmias, coronary artery disease, stroke, and heart failure. The picture that emerges is complicated, but generally positive. [...] All up, the new AHA statement concludes that there's a growing body of evidence that caffeine isn't harmful when taken in moderation, and that coffee specifically may be beneficial.

sentences.en: The statement focused on caffeine's relationship with cardiovascular risk factors, such as blood pressure and diabetes, as well as types of cardiovascular disease, including arrhythmias, coronary artery disease, stroke, and heart failure.
sentences.zh: 该声明重点关注咖啡因与心血管危险因素（如血压和糖尿病）以及心血管疾病类型（包括心律失常、冠状动脉疾病、中风和心力衰竭）的关系。

sentences.en: The picture that emerges is complicated, but generally positive. [...]
sentences.zh: 出现的情况很复杂，但总体上是积极的。 [...]

sentences.en: All up, the new AHA statement concludes that there's a growing body of evidence that caffeine isn't harmful when taken in moderation, and that coffee specifically may be beneficial.
sentences.zh: 总而言之，美国心脏协会的新声明得出的结论是，越来越多的证据表明，适量摄入咖啡因并无害处，而且咖啡可能特别有益。

content_html_zh: 美国心脏协会的一项新科学声明得出结论，每天最多约 400 毫克，或大约三到五杯纯咖啡，对大多数成年人来说是安全的，并且可能与降低心血管疾病的风险有关。 这些好处似乎在很大程度上取决于来源和制备方法，咖啡和茶看起来比能量饮料更受欢迎，而添加的糖、奶油、糖浆或甜味剂可能会抵消其优势。 ScienceAlert 报道称：“咖啡中的咖啡因是数百万人日常生活的重要组成部分，”该声明背后的美国心脏协会志愿者写作小组主席、加州大学旧金山分校心脏病专家格雷戈里·马库斯 (Gregory Marcus) 说道。 “在我们对最新研究的回顾中，对于大多数成年人来说，每天摄入最多 400 毫克咖啡因，相当于每天最多五杯不添加糖或填充物的含咖啡因咖啡，是安全的，不会增加心血管风险。” 该声明重点关注咖啡因与心血管危险因素（如血压和糖尿病）以及心血管疾病类型（包括心律失常、冠状动脉疾病、中风和心力衰竭）的关系。 出现的情况很复杂，但总体上是积极的。 [...] 总而言之，美国心脏协会的新声明得出的结论是，越来越多的证据表明，适量摄入咖啡因并无害处，而且咖啡可能特别有益。 该声明发表在《循环》杂志上。
